title: storage/ → store/ — contract/tx split + tests decomposition cut destiny under decisions.md
privilege: Architect cut contract only. decisions.md is sole Spec authority — this file yields on conflict. Current condemned paths appear only as Delete evidence; every seat and rewire destination is a target-tree path. Claim-tag inflation or jointly impossible meters void this plan. Pre-law destinies re-derived under the law; corrections recorded as rulings.

scope.this_cut: Condemned: crates/kyzo-core/src/storage/{mod,tests}.rs — the storage/ directory dies. Living targets: store/{contract,tx,fjall,backup,verify_walk}.rs; kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs (encoding-law battery); kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs (DST instrument battery); kyzo-trials/src/{crash,dst,conformance,fuzz}.rs.
scope.not_this_json: query/ (01), runtime/ (02), engines/ (04), data+fixed_rule (05), hosts (06). The storage-epic constructs (SweepDoor, WAL, CheckpointSeal, WriteAuthority …) are 07-storage-seats.json — this cut relocates the CURRENT contract, it does not build the Spec machine.
foundation_already_true: kyzo-trials/src/{conformance,dst,crash,serializability}.rs landed with real content (the story-#79 kit lives at kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs)
storage/{conformance,crash_matrix,sim}.rs already relocated in prior executed cuts
scope.target_not_current: by_target and allowlist.targets are target-tree paths only.

authority: This file — cut destiny; subordinate to decisions.md

claim_tags.law: Mandatory Spec tags from decisions.md only. Every unrepresentable re-tags under exactly one. Inflating Unexposed to Unconstructible voids this plan.
claim_tags.Unconstructible: No path through the sealed Rust type and crate boundary constructs the illegal state. Proof: private constructor + compile-fail a crate-consumer trybuild can demonstrate.
claim_tags.Refused: Reachable program; typed runtime outcome. Named refuse variants.
claim_tags.Unexposed: Absent from the public API but reachable by a hostile adapter. Name residual risk; real detection or residual accepted.

joint_satisfaction.law: Allowlist ⊇ every TARGET file the meters require to change. Condemned paths are deleted via delete_after — not edited as living seats.
joint_satisfaction.proof_pairing: compile-fail for signature-enforced laws (snapshot-then-mint, consuming commit); grep-delete for path/symbol absence.
joint_satisfaction.no_condemned_rewire: storage/* paths appear only under deletes/delete_after.

deletes.law: E-action ledger for condemned CURRENT paths.

paths.why: Contract prose + Storage trait + FormatVersion + SystemClock + seal → store/contract.rs; ReadTx/WriteTx/ConflictError → store/tx.rs; module decls are structural glue dying with the directory.

One tier-wide test file the target decomposes beside its subjects: encoding-law battery → kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs; fjall pins → store/fjall.rs + store/contract.rs tests; backup battery → store/backup.rs; verify pair → store/verify_walk.rs; process-abort crash → kyzo-trials/src/crash.rs; DST semantics + seven campaigns + hardening sentinel → kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs with campaign shapes feeding kyzo-trials/src/dst.rs. The generic kit-source duplicates die in favor of the conformance kit call.

symbols.name: per-backend copies of the generic KV/MVCC/RYOW/del_range/phantom/concurrent-writers/chunk-boundary/send-sync scenarios
symbols.why: CONDEMNED AS SUPERSEDED: they are the story-#79 kit's extraction source, verbatim-duplicated by kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs generic laws which fjall and sim already pass via run_full_battery. Only what the kit deliberately excludes (the per-backend pins) survives.

delete_guarded_invariants.name: no second conformance battery
delete_guarded_invariants.close: rg 'run_full_battery' crates → definitions only in kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs

not_deletes.item: the write_write_race_aborts_second_committer pin and its sim twin
not_deletes.why: The contract-v2 pin carrying v1 history, re-pinned KNOWINGLY — the two must stay together; they are per-backend pins the kit deliberately excludes.
Contract-semantics pin, travels with the v2 pin pair.

not_deletes.item: SCOPE HONESTY doc on process-abort vs power-cut
not_deletes.why: Abort simulates process crash; a power cut is a stronger event needing fault-injection infrastructure — the doc is the honesty gate for §29's future campaign and travels to kyzo-trials/src/crash.rs verbatim.

meters: rg -n 'fn as_of_oracle' crates/kyzo-core/src/store → at least 1 (time-travel oracle arm re-homed beside its cursors)

refused.law: Typed runtime outcomes. Named variants. Authority honest.

items.name: write-write race — second committer
items.authority: cut_destiny — sealed contract history v2 (KyzoDB sides with PostgreSQL SSI / TiKV: abort write-write races)
items.why: Retry-on-conflict is a control-flow decision, not a string match; a blind put cannot silently swallow a concurrent insert.

items.name: corrupt key during a skip scan
items.variant: typed decode error WITHOUT cursor advance
items.why: A scan cannot silently step over bytes it could not judge.

items.name: dump advertising a floor below its own rows
items.variant: dump floor refusal (row stamped above its own floor)
items.why: A backup that lies about its floor forges restore-time clock truth.

items.name: restore into a non-empty store
items.authority: cut_destiny — restore_storage is whole-store-into-empty by contract
items.why: Partial merge-restore is a second write door for forged history (§80 shape).

items.name: pre-value-plane FormatVersion v4 store open
items.variant: FormatVersion mismatch refusal naming the stamp
items.why: The #119 migration boundary made explicit and executable: v4 SPECIFICALLY refused, CURRENT parses as exactly 5; older stamps still parse so the mismatch refusal can NAME them.

unexposed.law: Residual hostile-adapter risk with real detection or residual accepted.

items.name: fsync failure after commit application (commit_durable shortfall)
items.residual_risk: The transaction IS committed and crash-survivable in the process, but power-cut-lost; a host that ignores the durability shortfall error advertises durability it does not have.
items.detection: sim durability-tiers campaign arm (deterministic / failed-fsync / 200-seed mixed) distinguishes committed-crash-survivable from power-cut-lost; §29's power-cut DST campaign is the permanent license

items.name: hostile writer below the kernel injecting bytes into the ordered store
items.residual_risk: Raw puts below the session/catalog surface can plant undecodable or misplaced bytes.
items.detection: verify_storage campaign on a REAL kernel-written store: corruption surfaces as BadTag and the walk continues past the wound; catalog-aware per-format value verification is real, not decorative

carried_obligations.obligation: abort() simulates process crash only; the power-cut claim needs fault-injection infrastructure (kyzo-crashfs / dm-flakey class). Until §29's power-cut-at-commit-door campaign is green, the word 'durable' is not licensed in Spec claims.

kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs kit pass is necessary-not-sufficient for production Committed; the StableCommitCap arm requirement lands with 07-storage-seats.json. Record at the kit so nobody reads kit-green as durability license.

tests.law: Laws-not-scenarios: each battery lands beside its subject; generators justified by the exact mutant/bug shape they catch travel with that justification intact.

fate.from: storage/tests.rs encoding-law battery (corpus, laws 1–3, byte-flip harness, signed-zero, shared-boundary generative arm)
fate.to: crates/kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs — it tests the codec, not the store; the corpus doubles as fuzz-seed material for kyzo-trials/src/fuzz.rs

fate.from: fjall-specific pins (inverted-range poisoning, options/stats, FormatVersion stamps incl. v4 boundary, clock/watermark batteries, snapshot-then-mint reproducer, RAM-floor pin)
backup battery incl. both floor pins and edge cases (huge length prefix is an error not an allocation abort; truncation mid-pair; truncated floor field)
verify pair (real-kernel fixture; walk-continues-past-the-wound; corrupt-polarity value catch)
fate.to: crates/kyzo-core/src/store/verify_walk.rs module tests (fixture builds through the session crate's public surface after the split)
per-backend module tests beside their cursors (store/fjall.rs, kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs); the skip-walk theorem already owns the generic proof at store/skip_walk.rs
crates/kyzo-trials/src/crash.rs (real-process territory, same lane as the FUSE matrix)

fate.from: DST semantics tests + seven campaigns + hardening sentinel + retry-liveness
fate.to: crates/kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs (they are the instrument's own proof battery); campaign SHAPES feed crates/kyzo-trials/src/dst.rs

fate.die: superseded by kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs run_full_battery — the kit call replaces the copies

meters: rg -n 'law2' crates/kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs → exhaustive-pairwise order arm present
rg -n 'write_write_race_aborts_second_committer' crates/kyzo-core/src/store crates/kyzo-crashfs/src → exactly 2 (fjall pin + sim twin stay together)
rg -n 'concurrent_increments_lose_nothing_at_the_storage_layer' crates/kyzo-core/src/store/fjall.rs → exactly 1 (the named reproducer the snapshot-then-mint proof cites)

authoritative_constructs: Storage trait + sealed module + FormatVersion (CURRENT=5) + SystemClock — store/contract.rs
sim instrument + its proof battery — kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs
conformance kit (public law for second backends) — kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs

unconstructible.name: reads-from order disagreeing with stamp order (mint before snapshot)
unconstructible.proof: compile-fail by signature: the stamp mint takes the open snapshot as an argument, so the reverse order does not type-check for a crate consumer (the v3 SNAPSHOT-THEN-MINT proof)

unconstructible.name: use of a committed write transaction
unconstructible.proof: compile-fail (trybuild): consuming commit — a committed transaction is a value that no longer exists (existing write_tx_use_after_commit/abort suites)

rulings.a: The sealed contract history (v2 write-write abort with industry comparison; v3 mandatory bitemporality with the snapshot-then-mint proof and shadowed-forever lineage; v4 one-cursor skip scan; v4 bump-anyway: the decodable tag space is part of the format's identity) travels VERBATIM into store/contract.rs's doc. Lossy compression of these rulings is a plan violation.

Per decisions.md §48: the Store API carries no fjall types; fjall is one adapter at store/fjall.rs behind store/contract.rs. The sealed module admits the simulator as the contract's own test double, not a second backend. Per §85 the kit is public law for second backends — it lives at kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs and its pass is necessary-not-sufficient (carried obligation).

CORRECTION under the law: the current Storage contract has no WAL/SweepDoor/Committed-mint machinery (§21/§25). This cut does NOT invent them — it relocates the current contract honestly. The Spec machine is built by the storage epic per 07-storage-seats.json; conflating relocation with construction is how half-built doors ship.

The one law (binary order = semantic order) is model vocabulary; its battery seats at kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs, not beside the store. The store consumes lawful bytes; it does not own the order proof.

ban: Do not weaken any per-backend pin while replacing the generic copies with the kit call.
Do not separate the fjall v2 write-write pin from its sim twin.
Do not let the encoding-law corpus fork — one corpus at kyzo-model, referenced as fuzz seeds by trials.
Do not read kit-green as a durability license (§27/§85 — necessary-not-sufficient).
Do not claim power-cut durability from the process-abort test (scope honesty).
Do not add fjall types to the Storage trait (§48).
Do not use whole-engine cargo green as this cut's meter.
Do not invent a fourth Spec claim tag.

rewire: TARGET store/contract.rs ← contract prose + Storage trait + FormatVersion + SystemClock + seal from condemned storage/mod.rs
TARGET store/tx.rs ← ReadTx/WriteTx/ConflictError from condemned storage/mod.rs
TARGET kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs ← encoding-law battery from condemned storage/tests.rs
TARGET kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs ← DST battery from condemned storage/tests.rs
TARGET kyzo-trials/src/crash.rs ← process-abort test from condemned storage/tests.rs

proof_meters: compile-fail by signature: stamp mint requires the open snapshot argument (snapshot-then-mint)
compile-fail (trybuild): write transaction unusable after commit/abort (consuming commit)

crates/kyzo-core/src/store/contract.rs.kind: TARGET — the storage contract: ordered scans, SSI, consuming commits
owns: sealed module (one backend by decree; sim as the contract's own test double)
ban: second backend admitted outside the sealed module without kit + (future) StableCommitCap arm
carried_notes: Written for that machine, not for any historical backend's shape; two-species genus with consuming commit — a committed transaction is not an invalid state to guard against but a value that no longer exists.

Concurrency economics stated plainly: reads AND writes are the conflict surface; first-committer-wins; uniqueness is enforced by the write itself; batch_put outside the surface; parallel preparation with serial commit application as the throughput ceiling; long-reader GC delay; coarse as-of ranges in write transactions.
Sealed contract history v2/v3/v4 verbatim (see rulings.contract-history-is-law).

FormatVersion CURRENT=5 with the whole version history in its doc; the v4 BUMP-ANYWAY ruling: the decodable tag space is part of the format's identity same as the tags already in it; canonical-spelling parse refusing non-canonical stamps.
SystemClock: max(now,last+1) CAS mint; floor/raise_floor for restore; an ABANDONED transaction's mint still raises the floor — a too-high floor is safe, a reused stamp is not.

crates/kyzo-core/src/store/tx.rs.kind: TARGET — transactions: snapshot isolation, typed conflicts
delete_meter: rg -n 'fn commit' crates/kyzo-core/src/store/tx.rs → consuming self signature
carried_notes: Degenerate ranges EMPTY never an error; whole-range conflict tracking on open — the conservative choice, and the one phantom protection needs; del_range's degenerate-tracks-nothing caveat.
range_skip_scan_tuple: mandatory two-axis with the corrupt-key error-without-advancing rule; keys-only scans so counts never pay value I/O.
system_stamp minted at snapshot creation; commit_durable's honest failure semantics — the transaction IS committed; the error reports the durability shortfall, not a rollback.

crates/kyzo-core/src/store/fjall.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — fjall adapter + its per-backend pins
carried_notes: inverted_ranges pin drives the store-poisoning regression through EVERY range entry point with contention arming commit-time validation, then proves the write-serialize lock unpoisoned.
RAM-floor pin: sane-band assertion catches a unit mixup without pinning an exact host-dependent value.

crates/kyzo-core/src/store/backup.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — dump/restore + floor law
carried_notes: The concurrency floor pin re-parses each dump's FILE BYTES independently — not the in-process values dump_storage itself computed — with the wait-for-writers scheduling-artifact guard; sabotage-verify runs with a legitimate-row control.
Edge cases: huge length prefix is an error, not an allocation abort; truncation mid-pair; truncated floor field.

crates/kyzo-core/src/store/verify_walk.r.kind: TARGET living seat — catalog-aware full ordered verification walk
ban: raw-puts fixtures for catalog-aware verification (the verifier needs the real entry taxonomy)
carried_notes: Corruption injected below the kernel surfaces as BadTag and THE WALK CONTINUES past the wound (quarantine-shaped precursor of §50); a still-decodable key over a corrupt polarity byte proves per-format value verification is real, not decorative.
After the split the real cataloged fixture builds through the session crate's public surface.

crates/kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs.kind: TARGET — the one-law encoding battery (laws, not scenarios)
ban: example-based order tests standing in for the pairwise law
carried_notes: Laws-not-scenarios: cross-type tag disorder, non-monotone float encodings, NaN order divergence are invisible to example-based tests; law2 is EXHAUSTIVE PAIRWISE so cross-type disagreements cannot hide behind sort stability and a failure names the exact offending pair.
corpus rules: every variant, ≥2 members each so cross-type AND within-type pairs exist; tricky regions enumerated; adding a case is one line; arb_value excludes regex FOR A REASON.
shared-boundary generative arm justified by the exact bug shape: independent i64 draws almost never share a boundary, so the generic generator has no power against a comparison that drops one field.

byte-flip harness: every single-byte mutation × three flip masks over every corpus encoding — the structured-corruption space the random Law 3 generator misses; the corpus doubles as fuzz-seed material for kyzo-trials/src/fuzz.rs.

crates/kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — the DST instrument + its own proof battery
owns: seven campaigns (retry survival; crash-at-every-point clean prefix; durability tiers; time travel under interleaved writers; write skew; no lost phantom; first-committer-wins)
ban: positional (global op-counter) fault plans (the sentinel exists to kill them)
carried_notes: Hardening sentinel: fixed logical work across 1/2/4/8 FREE-RUNNING OS threads, no scheduler; the (op, attempt)→outcome matrix byte-identical per seed and different across seeds, with both-arms anti-vacuity.

Write-skew campaign: overlapping snapshots with crossed read/write sets must abort at least one side in EVERY seed; final state one of the two SERIAL outcomes. Lost-phantom campaign observes commit order through the serialized scheduler with per-branch assertions.
The interleaving log key whose final content IS the commit order, and whose length proves no update was lost.
Retry-liveness: 90% storms; the missing-attempt-component mutant named as mutation-verified.

crates/kyzo-trials/src/crash.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — real-process crash territory
ban: claiming power-cut coverage from process abort

crates/kyzo-trials/src/dst.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — campaign shapes from the sim battery feed the DST lane
owns: campaign shapes registry fed by sim battery

crates/kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs.kind: TARGET living seat — the story-#79 kit; per §85 public law for second backends
ban: reading kit pass as production durability license (§27/§85)
delete_meter: rg -n 'necessary-not-sufficient|necessary not sufficient' crates/kyzo-trials/src/conformance.rs → doc note present

must_add: kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs description names the encoding-law battery ownership (corpus + laws 1–3)

final_meters: store/contract.rs and store/tx.rs exceed stub length; contract history v2/v3/v4 present in contract.rs doc
encoding-law battery present at kyzo-model/src/format/tests.rs (law1/law2/law3 + byte-flip)
sim seven campaigns + hardening sentinel present at kyzo-crashfs/src/sim.rs
fjall v2 pin + sim twin both present (exactly 2 hits)

seated_laws.law: snapshot-then-mint: reads-from order agrees with stamp order by signature
seated_laws.meter: the stamp-mint function signature takes the open snapshot; the named reproducer test (concurrent_increments_lose_nothing_at_the_storage_layer) passes at store/fjall.rs

seated_laws.law: a scan never silently steps over bytes it could not judge
seated_laws.meter: corrupt-key test asserts typed error without cursor advance

seated_laws.law: one conformance battery — backends prove themselves against the kit, not private copies
seated_laws.meter: rg -n 'run_full_battery' crates → call sites in fjall/sim tests; definitions only in conformance.rs

qae.q: The sim instrument battery lands in kyzo-crashfs (the instrument's crate), but the seven campaigns are contract-semantics proofs that also gate the fjall backend. If they live only beside the sim, what stops a future fjall change from passing while the sim-only campaigns still shine green?
qae.a: The campaigns run against the Storage trait, parameterized over backend, seated in kyzo-crashfs beside the sim but instantiated for BOTH backends where the contract permits (fjall skips only fault-injection arms that need the sim's injection hooks). The kit (conformance.rs) remains the both-backends floor; the campaigns are the sim-native ceiling.
qae.e: The soft alternative — 'sim campaigns cover the contract, fjall inherits by faith' — is trust-the-twin, a carriage-as-authority shape. Close: campaign functions generic over the sealed Storage trait; fjall instantiations for every arm that needs no injection hook; meter counts instantiations.

campaigns_proposed.campaign: kyzo-crashfs power-cut-at-commit-door: inject power cut (not process abort) at every commit-path point via the fault FS; every minted commit ack survives; crash-during-recovery converges idempotently. Green is the license for 'durable' in Spec claims; the process-abort test never substitutes.

dump bytes determinism: same committed history under controlled schedules produces byte-identical FormatVersioned dumps across two runs and two hosts; any divergence names an unseeded RNG or racy schedule in the sealed-artifact path.
